_comment: Per-family Spearman rho (knob rank vs radon metric), aggregated from bench/out/anchor.json per_sample. radon 6.0.1, MI multi=False. CC stays monotone-positive for every family; MI is monotone-negative for every family EXCEPT threshold_select (CONDITIONAL_SELECT), where MI is near-zero/insensitive because the programs are tiny and Halstead volume is frozen across knob levels (see diagnosis).

threshold_select_mi_diagnosis.cause: CONDITIONAL_SELECT programs are tiny and the spaghetti knob freezes Halstead volume. The clean baseline is a one-line ternary cascade; the spaghetti expansions add if/else+try/except control-flow scaffolding that reuses the operators/operands already present, so Halstead volume is constant across ALL knob levels (T2: 12.00; T4: 31.02). With volume pinned and CC pinned at the module level, MI is driven only by the small 16.2*ln(SLOC) term, and SLOC barely changes across light/standard/heavy/max, so MI saturates and ties. Worse, the clean baseline (SLOC=2) already sits near the MI ceiling and is LOWER than the minimal expansion, producing a rank inversion at clean->minimal that drives rho to ~0.
threshold_select_mi_diagnosis.note: By contrast, families whose expansion grows Halstead volume (allowlist clean->max volume 4.75->114.69; config_resolver 0->62.27) have clean, monotone MI (rho -0.986 / -0.845). CC stays monotone-positive everywhere (threshold_select CC rho = +0.655), so only the MI witness is insensitive for this one smallest family.
